name: Dense Tier-2 LoRA — same-repo hard negatives confirmed multi-seed (issue_to_fixing_pr)
hypothesis: Is R18's single-seed hardness gain (same-repo hard batching beats matched random, +0.017 ΔR@1) real across seeds, or MPS run-to-run noise?
leakage_checks: LoRA trains on train-repo fixes pairs only; eval on 32 held-out test repos; references scrubbed.
Hard negatives (for the deferred mined config) mined from the TRAIN split only — no test leakage.
Paired across seeds {0,1}: repo-hard ALWAYS beats matched random — gap +0.0188 (s0) / +0.0205 (s1), mean +0.0197, std 0.0009, above the ~+/-0.008 MPS noise.
known_limitations: Metrics shown are the best cell (repo-hard, seed 1, R@1 0.661); the multi-seed means are random +0.122 vs repo-hard +0.142 ΔR@1 vs the frozen 0.515 baseline.
Two seeds (light confirmation), chosen to fit an unstable MPS compute window; effect is tight (gap std 0.0009) so unlikely to overturn, but a 3rd-5th seed is the natural follow-up.

H2 (mined hard negatives beyond same-repo batching) is implemented + smoke-tested but DEFERRED — longer mined cells exceeded the environment's background-job time limit. Trained on Apple M5 GPU (MPS); exploratory.
